title: Global SSL Verification Disabled (MITM Risk)
description: The CLI script sets 'NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED = 0'. This disables certificate validation for all HTTPS requests in the process, making the tool vulnerable to Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks when communicating with Supabase or Google APIs.

title: Hardcoded Mock User ID in CLI Fallback
description: code-eye.js defaults to 'usr-1' if the JWT subject is missing. This could lead to data being associated with a non-existent or administrative user by accident.

title: Missing Error Handling in Concurrent Scans
description: Uploader.tsx uses a concurrency limit but catches errors per file only with a console.warn. If a critical API failure occurs (e.g. quota limit), the UI may show 'done' even if many files failed.
code_snippet: console.warn(`File analysis failed for ${file.name}, skipping.`, scanErr);

title: Inefficient Re-sorting in Real-time Updates
description: The real-time subscription in App.tsx re-sorts the entire issues array on every single INSERT. As the list grows, this becomes an O(N log N) operation on every socket message.
suggestion:
### AS-IS
```typescript
setIssues(prev => [payload.new as Issue, ...prev].sort((a, b) => b.priority_score - a.priority_score));
```
### TO-BE
```typescript
// Use a binary search insert or maintain the list in a sorted state to keep it O(N).
```
code_snippet: setIssues(prev => [payload.new as Issue, ...prev].sort((a, b) => b.priority_score - a.priority_score));
